title: How a Radar Satellite Tracks Russian Attacks on Ukraine's Power Grid
subtitle: Using Sentinel-1 SAR imagery to detect damage to energy infrastructure
blurb: An open-source pipeline using radar satellite imagery to detect and map Russian strikes on Ukraine's power grid, with three deep-dive case studies.

title: The Cyclone Harry on the Central Mediterranean Migration Route
subtitle: Reconstructing a storm's path and the shipwrecks that occurred during it
blurb: Combining ERA5 reanalysis data and Meteosat satellite imagery with migrant shipwreck records to reconstruct how a specific storm intersected with lives at sea.

title: Tracking Algerian Military Activity
subtitle: An original dataset of 143 weekly operational reports from Algeria's army
blurb: Scraping, OCR-ing, and geolocating 143 weekly Algerian military reports to build the first systematic dataset on migration enforcement in the Sahara.

title: The Caucasus Corridor
subtitle: How European-to-Asian flights rerouted after Russia closed its airspace
blurb: Tracking how flight corridors shifted to the Caucasus after 2022 and how GPS interference now threatens the new routes.

title: How Does an Election Forecast Model Work?
subtitle: An interactive explainer on zweitstimme.org's Bundestagswahl forecast
blurb: A scrollytelling walk-through of the zweitstimme.org forecast model, from poll aggregation to constituency prediction.

title: U.S. Territorial Expansion and Native American Land Loss
subtitle: A map-based narrative of displacement told through GIS data
blurb: Combining GIS records of Native American land cessions with U.S. territorial expansion shapefiles to challenge the 'American adventure' narrative.

title: Don't Trust the Polls. Don't Ignore Them Either.
subtitle: A scroll-driven explainer on poll literacy
blurb: Five common misconceptions about election polls, visualized with real data from the 2025 Bundestagswahl.

title: The Changing Face of Parliament
subtitle: Who represents Germany? A data portrait of the Bundestag, 1949–2022
blurb: Age, gender, religion, birthplace, profession — how Germany's parliament has changed over 75 years.

title: The Geography of Silence — Press Freedom in Türkiye 2015–2025
subtitle: A decade of press freedom violations mapped and categorized
blurb: 1,427 incidents of press freedom violations in Turkey, scraped from the Mapping Media Freedom database and categorized by type and geography.

title: The Parallel Record: Eagle Hills Georgia Ad Investigation
subtitle: What the Meta Ad Library reveals about who the developer is really selling to
blurb: If Eagle Hills is selling to Georgians, why are 93% of their ads in English and the resale market runs half in Russian?

title: Spring on Two Wheels
subtitle: Pedelecs have made cycling easier — but not safer
blurb: E-bikes have made cycling more accessible, but cyclist fatalities haven't followed the decline in overall road deaths.

title: Heat-Stressed India: The Seen and the Unseen
subtitle: How extreme heat intersects with maternal and child health across India's districts
blurb: Merging district-level heat risk indices with health survey data to identify India's double-jeopardy zones.

title: From Ice Cream to Politics: How Opinions Become Numbers
subtitle: An interactive explainer on polling methodology
blurb: Using a fictional ice cream survey to teach how electoral polls work, then connecting it to real German polling data.

title: Buying Access Through Education
subtitle: Gender, migration, and career outcomes among Hertie alumni
blurb: How gender and migration background intersect with career trajectories and compensation among Hertie School graduates.

title: Paper Money: How Not to Store Value
subtitle: A scroll-driven video essay on currency devaluation
blurb: A Blender-rendered, scroll-driven video on the long-run erosion of paper money's purchasing power.

title: The Efficiency Trap
subtitle: When supply chains run through too few places
blurb: From the Strait of Hormuz to rare earth magnets — how concentration risk makes global supply chains fragile.

title: Price Convergence in Europe
subtitle: Are Central and Eastern European prices catching up — and at what cost?
blurb: Tracking how price convergence in Europe accelerated during the inflation shock — and whether wages kept up.

title: Are Data Centres Driving Up Your Electricity Bill?
subtitle: Examining the relationship between data centre growth and electricity prices in Virginia
blurb: A scrollytelling investigation combining infrastructure data, wholesale electricity prices, and census tract matching to test whether data centres raise local energy costs.

title: Food Deserts in the United States
subtitle: Mapping exclusion and systemic inequality
blurb: Connecting historical redlining to present-day food deserts using USDA data and OSM-scraped retail locations.

title: Remigrazione: How a Word Entered Italian Parliament
subtitle: Tracking the journey of a far-right term from street slogan to governing-party policy
blurb: From neonazi street groups to the Italian parliamentary record in two years. A word's journey, tracked through every single mention.

title: Russia's Systematic Targeting of Ukraine's Power Grid
subtitle: Mapping three years of attacks on energy infrastructure
blurb: Seven waves of attacks on Ukraine's power plants and substations, mapped using conflict event data and OpenStreetMap infrastructure.

title: Berlin's First of May: A Map of Displacement
subtitle: How the route of the May Day demo traces who got pushed out
blurb: The demo route is a map of displacement. Tracing 40 years of Berlin's May Day protests through geography, arrests, and shifting neighborhoods.

title: The Invisible Battlefield: GPS Interference from Space
subtitle: How GPS jamming spread across four conflict zones
blurb: Scraping gpsjam.org to build a longitudinal dataset of GPS interference and tracking its spread across Ukraine, the Middle East, and the Baltic.

title: Berlin's Emergency Response: Your Neighborhood in Numbers
subtitle: An interactive tool exploring fire brigade response times across Berlin
blurb: Select your neighborhood, see your local emergency statistics, and compare to the rest of Berlin.

title: The Attention Economy of Comedy Scandals
subtitle: When comedians trend on Wikipedia, what kind of attention sticks?
blurb: Jokes sustain attention better than controversy. A data-driven look at what makes comedians trend.

title: Algeria's Migration Enforcement: Traces in Military Reports
subtitle: Scraping Algeria's Ministry of Defence to track migration-related arrests
blurb: Algeria refuses EU cooperation on migration data. But its own military publishes weekly arrest figures — if you know where to scrape.

title: America's Silent Killer: Heat Deaths in the United States
subtitle: How the official death toll obscures the true cost of extreme heat
blurb: Heat kills more Americans than hurricanes, tornadoes, and floods combined — but only if you count correctly.
